data.narrative:
Open Cosmos is a Growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Rafel Jorda Siquier, Jordi Barrera Ars, and Aleix Megias Homar. The company has raised $50M to date. The valuation universe consists of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

A single valuation method is applicable to this analysis. Stage-Progression triangulation uses the $50M total raised as a value proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding an implied value of $60M. Peer-Median analysis is not supportable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), and applying an all-sector median would lack specificity. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Open Cosmos does not disclose revenue, which is standard practice for private companies and prevents use of the Space & Aerospace public median P/S multiple of 25.8x across the five public tickers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, centered at $60M. This narrow range and single-method derivation reflect substantial data limitations. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable private transactions at identical stage and scale, undisclosed revenue preventing multiple-based approaches, and the nascent nature of commercial space operations, which limits historical precedent. A more precise valuation would require either disclosed financial metrics or identification of recent arm's-length transactions within the sector at comparable growth profiles.
